title: Avoid contradictory regex lookahead assertions
description: Contradictory lookahead assertions in regular expressions make patterns always fail to match, leading to dead code and unexpected behavior. This rule helps ensure regexes are logically correct and maintainable, reducing the risk of subtle bugs in pattern matching.
recommendation: Review and refactor regular expressions to remove or correct contradictory lookahead assertions so that patterns can match as intended.

noncompliant: Pattern.compile("(?=a)(?!a)") // This pattern can never match.
